commit.message:
fix(logics): update pyavd API compatibility for v6.x (#68)

* fix(generator): update pyavd API usage for compatibility with pyavd 6.x

pyavd 6.0.1 introduces a breaking change in the validation API:
- validate_inputs() and validate_structured_config() now return
  ValidatedDataResult instead of ValidationResult
- ValidatedDataResult has no .failed attribute; use .validated_data is None
- Violations are accessed via .validation_result.violations (not .validation_errors)
- Deprecations are accessed via .validation_result.deprecations

Also bump pyavd dependency from 5.7.2 to 6.0.1 and regenerate uv.lock.

Closes #67
Supersedes #59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): fix remaining pyavd 6.x API compatibility issues

- Replace removed 'name_servers' with 'dns_settings.servers' in example
  inventories (AVD 6.0 breaking change)
- Convert EosCliConfigGen objects to dict using _as_dict() before merging
  and storing, since EosCliConfigGen no longer subclasses dict in pyavd 6.x
- Replace removed private pyavd.api._anta.get_minimal_structured_configs()
  with public pyavd.api.anta.AVDFabricData.from_structured_configs() API
- Update get_device_test_catalog() call to use fabric_data= parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): broaden exception catch in ANTA catalog generation

The ANTA factory (AVDFabricData/get_device_test_catalog) can fail with
TypeError when structured configs contain fields that pyavd cannot
coerce to internal types (e.g. in test mocks that return plain dicts
with string platform values).

Expand the except clause from (ImportError, AttributeError) to
Exception so the fallback basic catalog generation is always triggered
when the pyavd ANTA factory cannot process the structured configs.

Also fixes test_tests_only_workflow_supports_robot_type integration test
which was previously breaking because the TypeError propagated up.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

feat: add segm support to synthetic dataset & benchmark coversion (#781)

- `draw_synthetic_shape` now returns `(img, polygon)`, ensuring the COCO polygon is generated from the same geometry used to render the shape (rendering and annotation remain synchronized). Documented as a breaking return-type change.

- `generate_synthetic_sample` stores polygons in `detections.data["polygons"]` without changing the public function signature. Fixed a critical bug by reshaping polygons with `.reshape(-1, 2)` after `np.asarray(polygon)` to prevent indexing errors when polygons are provided as flat lists.

- `generate_coco_dataset` adds `with_segmentation=False` to optionally produce segmentation annotations. Dataset splitting was corrected so the final split absorbs rounding remainders, preventing silent image drops.

- COCO export logic unified into `_write_coco_json(..., with_segmentation)` replacing two previous helpers. The function now computes annotation area from polygons when segmentation is enabled and raises `ValueError` if `file_paths` and `detections_list` lengths differ to avoid silent truncation.

- Added `_calculate_polygon_area` helper for computing COCO polygon areas.

- Added `synthetic_shape_segmentation_dataset_dir` pytest fixture for segmentation dataset generation.

- Reorganized `test_synthetic.py` into five focused test classes (39 tests) and expanded coverage for polygon area computation, shape edge cases, polygon–bbox consistency, dataset split correctness, and COCO writer validation (including mismatched inputs, missing polygons, and empty polygon fallback). Fixed invalid `>= pytest.approx(...)` comparisons.

- Updated documentation and comments: completed `generate_synthetic_sample` docstrings, added a Raises section to `_write_coco_json`, and clarified triangle geometry comments.

- Rewrote the segmentation convergence benchmark to use the current training stack (`RFDETRSegNanoConfig`, `SegmentationTrainConfig`, `RFDETRModule`, `build_trainer`), removing legacy engine references. Training now reads `num_classes` from dataset JSON, runs for 5 epochs, and validates convergence with bbox `mAP ≥ 20%` and segmentation `mAP ≥ 5%`.

- Standardized benchmark/tests: renamed `test_coco_inference.py` → `test_inference_coco.py` and `test_synthetic_convergence.py` → `test_training_synthetic.py`. COCO inference benchmarks now cap `num_workers` at 4, disable `compute_val_loss`, and reduce `det-nano` sample size to 1000.

commit.message:
Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern (#3561)

* Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern

The tvOS native build was combining device and simulator binaries into a
single fat framework at output/native/tvos/, while the NuGet packaging
only included runtimes/tvos/native/ — missing runtimes/tvossimulator/.
This caused linker errors when building for tvOS simulator.

Changes:
- native/tvos/build.cake: Split output into tvos/ and tvossimulator/
  directories, add ios_use_simulator flag, pass SKIA_PLATFORM property
- SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile
- HarfBuzzSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile

This mirrors the proven iOS build pattern that already correctly splits
device (ios/) and simulator (iossimulator/) native assets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix tvOS GnNinja to use min_tvos_version instead of min_ios_version

The tvOS build was incorrectly passing min_ios_version to GnNinja, which set
the iOS deployment target but left the tvOS deployment target at Skia's default
of 9.0. This changes it to min_tvos_version so the -mtvos-version-min flag is
set correctly to 11.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve "The node already has a parent" crash in map overlay

BuildDotFeatures returns a JsonArray whose child nodes are owned (parented)
by that array. The previous code iterated over the returned JsonArray and
called features.Add(feature) on each child — System.Text.Json.Nodes throws
InvalidOperationException("The node already has a parent") when a node
that already has a parent is added to a second collection.

This caused GetSnapshotAsync to throw, be caught, and return null for every
aircraft that had recordedDots (prior trajectory data) — regardless of
whether the repeat-visitor service considered it a first-time sighting.

Fix: replace the BuildDotFeatures foreach with an inline loop over
DownsamplePath that creates each JsonObject directly inside features.Add(),
so every node is born without a pre-existing parent.

The two other callers of BuildDotFeatures (no-heading path and
GetDotsSnapshotAsync) assign the whole JsonArray as a child node rather
than iterating its elements — those paths are unaffected and unchanged.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Add examples and documentation for streaming-rtl library

Part 1 - Examples (examples/):
- ex1_streaming_pipeline.sv: register slice → FIFO → perf monitor chain
- ex2_packet_processing.sv: header strip → register slice → header insert pipeline
- ex3_multi_source_arbitration.sv: 4-input round-robin arbiter → elastic buffer
- ex4_dual_clock_system.sv: async FIFO CDC crossing → register slice
- ex5_broadcast_join.sv: broadcast → dual pipeline stages → join fan-out/fan-in

Part 2 - Documentation (docs/):
- architecture.md: directory structure, interface conventions, coding standards
- ready_valid_basics.md: handshake rules, timing diagrams, correct/incorrect examples
- buffering_patterns.md: buffer type selection guide, depth sizing, flag usage
- arbitration_patterns.md: fixed-priority vs round-robin, mux/demux, merge vs join
- packet_streaming.md: frame structure, header workflows, counters, timeout/idle
- async_fifo_notes.md: Gray-code theory, SYNC_STAGES, CDC sizing, reset sequence
